process_name: Pair production of custodial charge-2/3 top partner T (T')
implementation_difficulty_reason: A faithful CR003 implementation needs collider reinterpretation data or an external tool chain plus an RS top-partner spectrum, width, and branching-ratio calculation. The repo currently has low-energy flavor filters and KK-gluon matching, not event-level acceptance or VLQ exclusion contours.

commands: rg -n "CR003|vector.?like|vectorlike|VLQ|Tprime|T.?prime|tprime|top.?partner|top partner|custodial.*partner|pair.?production|collider.?search|direct.?search" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "T_\{?5/3\}?|T5/3|X_\{?5/3\}?|charge.?2/3|charge.?5/3|KK.?gluon|Kaluza|resonance|heavy.?partner|partner.?mass" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n "M_KK|MKK|Mkk|KK|mass_bound|bound|constraint|check_" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n "collider|LHC|ATLAS|CMS|HEPData|cross.?section|luminosity|exclusion|CLs|95.?CL" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
findings: No direct match for CR003, VLQ, vector-like top partner, pair-production, collider-search, or direct-search strings in the required code paths.

Only adjacent KK/gluon strings appeared; for example quarkConstraints/couplings.py:1 and quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:1 are KK-gluon flavor-coupling / Delta F=2 modules, not collider top-partner filters.
quarkConstraints/modern/scan.py:1230-1255 constructs M_KK for scan points, and quarkConstraints/modern/scan.py:1492-1569 derives pass/fail lists only for epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0 systems.
quarkConstraints/modern/evaluation.py:643-666 builds M_KK-dependent KK-gluon matching and Delta F=2 summaries; no ATLAS/CMS/HEPData likelihood or top-partner mass observable is called.
scanParams/scan.py:399-429 records M_KK and scan metadata, while scanParams/scan.py:523-533 applies only the mu -> e gamma LFV filter in that legacy scan path.

post_2010_developments.why_it_matters: Early 13 TeV Run-2 Wb-targeted pair-production search with boosted W reconstruction; established the lepton+jets topology later used by the current ATLAS Wb result.
Extended coverage to Ht/Zt-rich final states with multiple b jets and boosted top/Higgs tags, broadening the branching-ratio coverage beyond Wb-targeted searches.
All-hadronic Run-2 search with neural-network W/Z/H/top jet classification; supplied an independent Ht-sensitive topology.
Full Run-2 Z-lepton search optimized for T -> Zt; PDG source for m_T > 1.60 TeV under B(T -> Zt)=1.
Full Run-2 CMS combination of single-lepton, same-sign dilepton, and multilepton channels; robust all-branching-mixture lower limit m_T > 1.48 TeV.
Current PDG-leading pure-Wb and isospin-singlet pair-production result: m_T > 1.70 TeV for B(T -> Wb)=1 and m_T > 1.36 TeV for the singlet branching pattern.

sections.process:
Collider RS resonance / partner search framing: CR003 is the direct LHC
search for a custodial charge-\(2/3\) top partner produced in pairs.  The
standard topology is
\[
  pp\to T\bar T\to tZ/tH/bW+X .
\]
This entry covers searches for pair production of a charge-\(2/3\)
vector-like top partner \(T\), often denoted \(t'\) in PDG listings, with
decays to third-generation quarks and electroweak or Higgs bosons.
sections.pdg_prose:
The current PDG Live \(Q009TPP\) listing records the relevant pair-production
mass exclusions.  The canonical 95\% CL entries for CR003 are:
\[
\begin{array}{lll}
\hline
\hbox{Assumption} & \hbox{Observed lower limit} & \hbox{Primary source} \\
\hline
{\cal B}(T\to Wb)=1 & m_T>1.70~\mathrm{TeV} &
\hbox{ATLAS 2024, PDG AAD 24N} \\
{\cal B}(T\to Wb:Ht:Zt)=1/2:1/4:1/4 & m_T>1.36~\mathrm{TeV} &
\hbox{ATLAS 2024, PDG AAD 24N} \\
{\cal B}(T\to Zt)=1 & m_T>1.60~\mathrm{TeV} &
\hbox{ATLAS 2023, PDG AAD 23AV} \\
{\cal B}(T\to Ht)=1 & m_T>1.50~\mathrm{TeV} &
\hbox{CMS 2023, PDG TUMASYAN 23V} \\
\hbox{all third-generation decay mixtures} & m_T>1.48~\mathrm{TeV} &
\hbox{CMS 2023} \\
\hline
\end{array}
\]
The strict metadata for these mass-exclusion values is in
\texttt{CR003.yaml} under \texttt{pdg\_or\_equivalent}; luminosity,
\(\sqrt{s}\), and channel details are kept as supporting inputs rather than
promoted observables.
sections.relevance:
CR003 constrains the physical mass of a charge-\(2/3\) custodial fermion
partner, not the KK-gluon mass or the flavor-anarchy matching scale by itself.
For QCD pair production the production rate is comparatively model-independent,
but the acceptance and mass limit depend on the assumed branching fractions
among \(Wb\), \(Zt\), and \(Ht\).  A custodial-RS model can map the lightest
top-partner mass and branching ratios to the 5D fermion representation, IR
localization, and electroweak embedding; absent that map, the LHC number is a
direct-search cross-check rather than a universal RS-flavor bound.

The current low-energy quark-flavor scan already quotes
\(M_{KK}^{\min}(p50,g_\ast=3)=47.26~\mathrm{TeV}\) in
\texttt{docs/quark\_scan\_methodology\_note.tex}.  The \(T\)-partner direct
limits above sit in the \(1.36\)--\(1.70~\mathrm{TeV}\) range, so they do not
compete with the anarchic-flavor \(M_{KK}\) envelope.  Their value is instead
to test non-anarchic corners, spectra with light custodial fermions, or models
where collider partners are not tied tightly to the flavor-setting KK scale.
sections.post_2008:
The Contino--Servant top-partner paper set the custodial-partner collider
motivation before LHC data.  The LHC search history then moved from early
same-sign and lepton+jets top-partner searches to full-Run-2
branching-fraction scans.  ATLAS arXiv:1707.03347 established a 13 TeV
lepton+jets \(Wb\)-targeted search with boosted \(W\) reconstruction.  ATLAS
arXiv:1803.09678 and arXiv:1808.01771 broadened the coverage to \(Ht\),
\(Zt\), multi-\(b\), and all-hadronic final states.  ATLAS arXiv:2210.15413
used the full Run-2 \(Z\)-lepton channel and gives the strongest pure-\(Zt\)
row in the PDG table.  CMS arXiv:2209.07327 combined single-lepton,
same-sign dilepton, and multilepton channels and gives the robust
all-decay-mixture exclusion.  ATLAS arXiv:2401.17165 is the current
PDG-leading pure-\(Wb\) and singlet \(Wb:Ht:Zt\) source.
sections.validity:
These limits assume prompt pair production of narrow vector-like quarks and
specific decay-mode hypotheses.  The common simplified-model assumption is
that \({\cal B}(T\to Wb)+{\cal B}(T\to Zt)+{\cal B}(T\to Ht)=1\).  Exotic
decays, sizable widths, altered electroweak couplings, long-lived partners, or
compressed spectra can invalidate a direct reading of the quoted mass limits.
Single-production searches can reach higher masses for chosen couplings, but
they are not the CR003 observable because the assigned process is pair
production.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  Targeted greps across \texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/},
\texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/},
\texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, and
\texttt{tests/} found no implementation of VLQ \(T\)-pair collider limits,
ATLAS/CMS direct-search likelihoods, HEPData exclusion contours, or a
top-partner mass filter.  Adjacent evidence is limited to KK-scale and
flavor-filter plumbing: \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/scan.py:1230}
constructs \texttt{M\_KK} for scan points, \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/scan.py:1492}
builds failing-system lists from flavor ratios, \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/scan.py:1503}
restricts those systems to \(\epsilon_K,K,B_d,B_s,D^0\), and
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/evaluation.py:643} starts the \(M_{KK}\)
resolution used for KK-gluon matching.  The current quark scope is summarized
as MFV fitting at \texttt{quarkConstraints/README.md:34}, KK-scale conventions
at \texttt{quarkConstraints/README.md:36}, and a \(\Delta F=2\) exclusion
slice at \texttt{quarkConstraints/README.md:38}; none of those lines is a
collider top-partner filter.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  A faithful live constraint needs either a simplified
acceptance map over \({\cal B}(Wb),{\cal B}(Zt),{\cal B}(Ht)\) or an external
reinterpretation workflow such as CheckMATE, MadAnalysis5, SModelS, or a
collaboration likelihood/HEPData grid.  It also needs an RS spectrum model
that predicts the lightest custodial \(T\) mass, width, and branching
fractions.  The current code has neither collider-event machinery nor a
top-partner spectrum/decay module.

matches.context:
L21: AAD 24N is based on 140 fb^-1 of pp data at sqrt(s) = 13 TeV. Pair
L22: production of vector-like t' is searched for in the lepton+jets mode. The
L23: data are consistent with the SM background predictions and limits are
L24: obtained for different branching ratios. The >1700 GeV limit is for t' in a
L25: weak-isospin doublet; the same limits also apply to t' of charge -4/3. The
L26: >1360 GeV limit is for a singlet model.

L28: include multiple top quarks.
L29:
L30: The limits obtained in this search are the strongest limits to date for
L31: T Tbar production, excluding masses below 1.48 TeV for all decays to
L32: third-generation quarks, and are the strongest limits to date for B Bbar
L33: production with B quark decays to tW.

priority_rationale_short: Top-partner T limits test light custodial spectra.
priority_rationale_full:
Charge-2/3 top-partner pair limits reach about 1.36-1.70 TeV depending on
branching fractions. They are relevant for custodial spectra with light
fermion partners, but they do not compete with low-energy anarchic flavor
and need model-specific branching and width mapping. This gives them
meaningful subleading power.
